states.name: NoActiveAccount
states.description: No user account is currently active for this Content App endpoint.

states.name: ActiveAccount_NoNodeBindings
states.description: A user account is active for this Content App, but no Node-specific access bindings are currently tracked.

states.name: ActiveAccount_WithNodeBindings
states.description: A user account is active and at least one Node has been granted access via Login(Node).

states.name: RateLimitExceeded
states.description: Rate limit exceeded for GetSetupPIN or Login requests in the current 10 minute window for the applicable scope.

transitions.timing_requirements: Server enforces ≤10 unique GetSetupPIN requests per 10 minutes per scope; this transition abstracts the block condition.
transitions.description: GetSetupPIN request rejected due to rate limiting; FAILURE status is returned and rate_limit_block_active is set.

transitions.timing_requirements: Server handles request normally, returning either non-empty or empty SetupPIN based on account match and temp ID validity.
transitions.description: GetSetupPIN processed when rate limit not exceeded; does not change active account state in this cluster model.

transitions.timing_requirements: Same 10 unique requests / 10 minutes constraint applies.
transitions.description: GetSetupPIN rejected due to rate limiting when an account is already active.

transitions.timing_requirements: Server may return non-empty SetupPIN only if TempAccountIdentifier maps to the same active account and is not expired.
transitions.description: GetSetupPIN handled without affecting active account state.

transitions.timing_requirements: Same rate limit applies regardless of Node bindings.
transitions.description: GetSetupPIN rejected due to rate limiting while account and Node bindings exist.

transitions.timing_requirements: Normal GetSetupPIN behavior; does not modify account or Node bindings.
transitions.description: GetSetupPIN handled without changing state.

transitions.timing_requirements: While rate_limit_block_active is TRUE, additional unique GetSetupPIN requests remain blocked.
transitions.description: Further GetSetupPIN attempts remain blocked under active rate-limit condition.

transitions.timing_requirements: Abstracts expiry of the 10 minute rate-limit window for GetSetupPIN and Login.
transitions.description: Rate limit window ends with no active account; unblocks further requests.

transitions.timing_requirements: Rate limit window expiry with active account but no Node-specific bindings.
transitions.description: Rate limit reset; returns to state with active account but no Node bindings.

transitions.timing_requirements: Rate limit window expiry with active account and Node bindings.
transitions.description: Rate limit reset; returns to state with active account and Node bindings.

transitions.timing_requirements: Server enforces ≤10 unique Login requests per 10 minutes per applicable scope.
transitions.description: Login rejected due to rate limiting when no account is active.

transitions.timing_requirements: Temporary Account Identifier lifetime ≤10 minutes; expired identifiers cause FAILURE.
transitions.description: Login fails because Temporary Account Identifier is expired; no state change.

transitions.guard_condition: rate_limiter_too_many_unique_requests == FALSE && temp_id_expired == FALSE && lookup_account_success == FALSE
transitions.timing_requirements: Login fails when no account is mapped to the given TempAccountIdentifier.
transitions.description: Login rejected because no account exists for the TempAccountIdentifier.

transitions.guard_condition: rate_limiter_too_many_unique_requests == FALSE && temp_id_expired == FALSE && lookup_account_success == TRUE && setup_pin_valid == FALSE
transitions.timing_requirements: Login requires a current active Setup PIN for the account; invalid PIN causes FAILURE.
transitions.description: Login rejected due to invalid or non-current Setup PIN.

transitions.guard_condition: rate_limiter_too_many_unique_requests == FALSE && temp_id_expired == FALSE && lookup_account_success == TRUE && setup_pin_valid == TRUE && policy_allow_auto_login == FALSE
transitions.timing_requirements: Server may validate credentials but not automatically set active account if policy disallows auto-login.
transitions.description: Login validation succeeds but active account is not changed because auto-login is not allowed; still respond SUCCESS.

transitions.guard_condition: rate_limiter_too_many_unique_requests == FALSE && temp_id_expired == FALSE && lookup_account_success == TRUE && setup_pin_valid == TRUE && policy_allow_auto_login == TRUE && node_present == FALSE
transitions.timing_requirements: Mapping service enforces aggressive timeout for TempAccountIdentifier and SetupPIN after successful Login.
transitions.description: Login succeeds, active account is set, no Node binding provided.

transitions.guard_condition: rate_limiter_too_many_unique_requests == FALSE && temp_id_expired == FALSE && lookup_account_success == TRUE && setup_pin_valid == TRUE && policy_allow_auto_login == TRUE && node_present == TRUE
transitions.timing_requirements: Server grants content app access to the given Node for the active account.
transitions.description: Login succeeds, active account is set and access is granted to a specific Node.

transitions.timing_requirements: Same Login rate limit applies even when an account is already active.
transitions.description: Login rejected due to rate limiting with an already active account.

transitions.timing_requirements: Expired Temporary Account Identifier causes Login FAILURE.
transitions.description: Login fails for expired temp ID; active account unchanged.

transitions.guard_condition: rate_limiter_too_many_unique_requests == FALSE && temp_id_expired == FALSE && lookup_account_success == FALSE
transitions.timing_requirements: Missing account mapping causes Login FAILURE.
transitions.description: Login fails for missing account; active account unchanged.

transitions.guard_condition: rate_limiter_too_many_unique_requests == FALSE && temp_id_expired == FALSE && lookup_account_success == TRUE && setup_pin_valid == FALSE
transitions.timing_requirements: Invalid or non-current Setup PIN causes Login FAILURE.
transitions.description: Login fails for invalid Setup PIN; active account unchanged.

transitions.guard_condition: rate_limiter_too_many_unique_requests == FALSE && temp_id_expired == FALSE && lookup_account_success == TRUE && setup_pin_valid == TRUE && policy_allow_auto_login == FALSE
transitions.timing_requirements: Auto-login disabled; server may still return SUCCESS but not change active account.
transitions.description: Login validation succeeds but policy forbids changing active account; remain with same active_account.

transitions.guard_condition: rate_limiter_too_many_unique_requests == FALSE && temp_id_expired == FALSE && lookup_account_success == TRUE && setup_pin_valid == TRUE && policy_allow_auto_login == TRUE && node_present == FALSE
transitions.timing_requirements: Auto-login updates active account if a different account is validated with no Node field.
transitions.description: Login succeeds and sets active account (possibly changing it), still no Node bindings.

transitions.guard_condition: rate_limiter_too_many_unique_requests == FALSE && temp_id_expired == FALSE && lookup_account_success == TRUE && setup_pin_valid == TRUE && policy_allow_auto_login == TRUE && node_present == TRUE
transitions.timing_requirements: Grant access to the specific Node after successful Login.
transitions.description: Login succeeds, sets active account and creates first Node binding.

transitions.timing_requirements: Login rate limiting applies regardless of existing bindings.
transitions.description: Login rejected due to rate limiting while Node bindings exist.

transitions.timing_requirements: Expired Temporary Account Identifier causes Login FAILURE.
transitions.description: Login fails for expired temp ID; active account and Node bindings remain unchanged.

transitions.guard_condition: rate_limiter_too_many_unique_requests == FALSE && temp_id_expired == FALSE && lookup_account_success == FALSE
transitions.timing_requirements: Missing account mapping causes Login FAILURE.
transitions.description: Login fails for missing account; active account and Node bindings remain unchanged.

transitions.guard_condition: rate_limiter_too_many_unique_requests == FALSE && temp_id_expired == FALSE && lookup_account_success == TRUE && setup_pin_valid == FALSE
transitions.timing_requirements: Invalid Setup PIN causes Login FAILURE.
transitions.description: Login fails for invalid Setup PIN; active account and Node bindings remain unchanged.

transitions.guard_condition: rate_limiter_too_many_unique_requests == FALSE && temp_id_expired == FALSE && lookup_account_success == TRUE && setup_pin_valid == TRUE && policy_allow_auto_login == FALSE
transitions.timing_requirements: Validation without auto-login does not modify bindings.
transitions.description: Login validation succeeds but policy prevents changing active account; state remains with Node bindings.

transitions.guard_condition: rate_limiter_too_many_unique_requests == FALSE && temp_id_expired == FALSE && lookup_account_success == TRUE && setup_pin_valid == TRUE && policy_allow_auto_login == TRUE && node_present == TRUE
transitions.timing_requirements: On success, server may update active account and ensure Node binding exists for the provided Node.
transitions.description: Login succeeds with Node; active account set and at least one Node binding remains present.

transitions.guard_condition: rate_limiter_too_many_unique_requests == FALSE && temp_id_expired == FALSE && lookup_account_success == TRUE && setup_pin_valid == TRUE && policy_allow_auto_login == TRUE && node_present == FALSE
transitions.timing_requirements: Auto-login with no Node may clear previous Node bindings modelled as aggregate.
transitions.description: Login succeeds without Node; active account set but Node bindings are not tracked in this abstraction.
Logout when no account is active; responds success and has no effect.
Logout clears the active account; Node parameter does not change outcome in this aggregate model; LoggedOut event emitted with Node or null.

transitions.guard_condition: node_present == TRUE && logout_clears_only_one_node == TRUE && other_nodes_remain == TRUE
transitions.timing_requirements: Content App MAY use Node to track access per Node; in this abstraction we keep exists_node_binding TRUE if at least one Node still has access.
transitions.description: Logout for a specific Node when other Node bindings remain; active account not globally cleared; LoggedOut event emitted with Node.

transitions.timing_requirements: When Logout is global (no Node), state becomes NoActiveAccount.
transitions.description: Global Logout clears active account and all Node bindings; LoggedOut event emitted with Node null when global.

transitions.timing_requirements: When implementation clears all access for this account on Logout(Node), state becomes NoActiveAccount.
transitions.description: Logout(Node) clears active account and all Node bindings in implementations that treat it as global for the account.

transitions.timing_requirements: Login attempts remain blocked during rate-limit window.
transitions.description: Login calls remain rejected while rate_limit_block_active is TRUE.

security_properties.description: A GetSetupPIN request that is accepted must not violate the 10 unique requests per 10 minutes rate limit.
security_properties.source: timing: GetSetupPIN rate limit ≤10 unique requests per 10 minutes

security_properties.description: A Login request that is accepted must not violate the 10 unique requests per 10 minutes rate limit.
security_properties.source: timing: Login rate limit ≤10 unique requests per 10 minutes

security_properties.description: GetSetupPIN responses with non-empty SetupPIN require that the Temporary Account Identifier was not expired.
security_properties.source: effect_on_receipt and timing: Temporary Account Identifier valid ≤10 minutes

security_properties.description: Login success requires that the Temporary Account Identifier used was not expired.
security_properties.source: effect_on_receipt: Login checks temp_id_expired before success

security_properties.description: Any successful Login must be preceded by validation of a current active Setup PIN for that account.
A non-empty SetupPIN in GetSetupPINResponse implies the requested and active accounts match and identifier is valid.
security_properties.source: effect_on_receipt: non-empty SetupPIN only when requested and active accounts match and temp ID not expired

security_properties.description: If accounts do not match or temp identifier is invalid, GetSetupPINResponse carries an empty SetupPIN.
security_properties.source: effect_on_receipt: else send GetSetupPINResponse with empty SetupPIN

security_properties.description: A LoggedOut event must cause the Fabric Admin to revoke access for the Node or all non-Admin Nodes to the Content App.
security_properties.source: event summary: Fabric Admin SHALL remove access on LoggedOut

definitions.definition: TYPE: abstract account reference or null. VALUES: null (no active account) or a specific user account identifier. DEFAULT: null. Represents the account currently active for the Content App endpoint.
definitions.usage_context: State invariants and transitions distinguishing NoActiveAccount vs ActiveAccount_* states.

definitions.definition: TYPE: boolean. VALUES: TRUE, FALSE. DEFAULT: FALSE. TRUE means at least one Node has been granted access to the Content App via a successful Login with Node present.
definitions.usage_context: Used to differentiate ActiveAccount_NoNodeBindings from ActiveAccount_WithNodeBindings.

definitions.definition: TYPE: boolean. VALUES: TRUE, FALSE. DEFAULT: FALSE. TRUE abstracts that the server is currently blocking further unique GetSetupPIN/Login requests due to exceeding the configured rate limit.
definitions.usage_context: Invariants in RateLimitExceeded state and guards on GetSetupPIN/Login transitions.

definitions.definition: TYPE: boolean condition. TRUE when the number of unique GetSetupPIN or Login requests in the last 10 minutes for the applicable scope exceeds the allowed maximum (≤10).
definitions.usage_context: Guard conditions on transitions handling GetSetupPIN and Login to model FAILURE responses due to rate limiting.

definitions.definition: TYPE: boolean condition. TRUE when the Temporary Account Identifier age exceeds its maximum allowed lifetime (≤10 minutes).
definitions.usage_context: Login transitions: if TRUE, Login fails and state does not move to success states.

definitions.definition: TYPE: boolean condition. TRUE if lookup_account_by_temp_id(TempAccountIdentifier) returned a non-null account.
definitions.usage_context: Login success path requires lookup_account_success == TRUE.

definitions.definition: TYPE: boolean condition. TRUE if has_current_active_setup_pin(acct, SetupPIN) for the account associated with the Temporary Account Identifier.
definitions.usage_context: Login transitions; must be TRUE for LoginSuccess.

definitions.definition: TYPE: boolean policy flag. TRUE if the Content App's policy allows automatically making the user account active upon successful credential validation.
definitions.usage_context: Login transitions decide whether to set active_account or not based on this flag.

definitions.definition: TYPE: boolean condition. TRUE if the optional Node field is present in the Login or Logout command payload.
definitions.usage_context: Transitions to decide whether Node-specific binding or global logout is applied.

definitions.definition: TYPE: boolean abstraction. TRUE when a Logout(Node) call is interpreted as revoking access only for that Node while keeping other bound Nodes.
definitions.usage_context: Logout transition from ActiveAccount_WithNodeBindings that remains in same state when other_nodes_remain is TRUE.

definitions.definition: TYPE: boolean abstraction. TRUE if, after processing a Logout(Node), at least one other Node still has access to the Content App for the active account.
definitions.usage_context: Guard condition to remain in ActiveAccount_WithNodeBindings after Logout(Node).

definitions.definition: TYPE: boolean abstraction. TRUE when a particular Logout invocation (Node present or not) is implemented to clear all Node access for the active account, not just the specified Node.
definitions.usage_context: Logout transition that moves to NoActiveAccount and sets exists_node_binding := FALSE.

definitions.definition: TYPE: abstract account reference. VALUE: the account obtained via lookup_account_by_temp_id(TempAccountIdentifier).
definitions.usage_context: Actions on successful Login transitions that set active_account := account_from_temp_id.

definitions.definition: Client ⇒ server command. FIELD: TempAccountIdentifier (string length 16–100). EFFECT: If rate limit not exceeded and account matches active account and temp ID not expired, server returns non-empty SetupPIN; otherwise empty string or FAILURE.
definitions.usage_context: Modeled as transitions that may set rate_limit_block_active or leave state unchanged but influence security properties.

definitions.definition: Server ⇒ client command. FIELD: SetupPIN (string; length ≥8 if non-empty; empty string indicates no match).
definitions.usage_context: Used in security properties to connect non-empty SetupPIN to account and identifier validity.

definitions.definition: Client ⇒ server command. FIELDS: TempAccountIdentifier (string 16–100), SetupPIN (string length ≥8), Node (optional node-id). EFFECT: On valid, current Setup PIN and non-expired temp ID and policy_allow_auto_login, MAY set active_account and grant Node access.
definitions.usage_context: Central command for transitions into ActiveAccount_* states and for authentication properties.

definitions.definition: Client ⇒ server command. FIELD: Node (optional node-id). EFFECT: Clears current user account or only the specified Node’s access depending on implementation; emits LoggedOut event.
definitions.usage_context: Transitions from ActiveAccount_* states to NoActiveAccount or within ActiveAccount_WithNodeBindings.

definitions.definition: EVENT. FIELD: Node (optional node-id). Emitted when current user logs out; Fabric Admin SHALL remove access for that Node or all non-Admin Nodes if Node absent.
definitions.usage_context: Referenced in security property SP8 to require access revocation after event emission.

definitions.definition: TYPE: predicate. TRUE when the represented SetupPIN is non-empty (has length ≥1).
definitions.usage_context: Used in conditional security properties SP3 and SP6.

definitions.definition: TYPE: predicate. TRUE when the represented SetupPIN is empty (zero-length string).
definitions.usage_context: Used in SP7 to state that invalid requests must yield an empty SetupPIN.

definitions.definition: TYPE: predicate. TRUE when the Temporary Account Identifier has not exceeded its 10 minute validity window at the time of use.
definitions.usage_context: Consequence in SP3 for GetSetupPIN non-empty responses.

definitions.definition: TYPE: predicate. TRUE when the account referenced by the Temporary Account Identifier equals the currently active account for the Content App.
definitions.usage_context: Consequence in SP6 linking non-empty SetupPIN to an account match.

definitions.definition: TYPE: predicate. TRUE when either the Temporary Account Identifier does not map to the active account or the identifier is invalid/expired.
definitions.usage_context: Condition in SP7 requiring an empty SetupPIN.

definitions.definition: EVENT. Indicates the start of a counting window for rate limiting unique requests for a given scope (e.g., client or Node).
definitions.usage_context: Required event in SP1 and SP2 to relate accepted requests to a rate-limit window.

definitions.definition: EVENT. Indicates the server has accepted and processed a GetSetupPIN request (including sending a response) rather than rejecting it due to rate limiting or other errors.
definitions.usage_context: Trigger_event in SP1.

definitions.definition: EVENT. Indicates the server did not reject a Login request due to rate limit or immediate validation errors (includes both success and policy-allowed no-op cases).
definitions.usage_context: Trigger_event in SP2.

definitions.definition: EVENT. Indicates that a Login resulted in a SUCCESS response and, when policy_allow_auto_login is TRUE, that the account credentials were validated.
definitions.usage_context: Trigger_event in SP4 and SP5.

definitions.definition: EVENT. Indicates that at the time a Temporary Account Identifier was used, it was within its maximum allowed lifetime.
definitions.usage_context: Required_event in SP4.

definitions.definition: EVENT. Indicates that the server verified the provided SetupPIN as the current active Setup PIN for the account.
definitions.usage_context: Required_event in SP5.

definitions.definition: EVENT. Abstracts the emission of the LoggedOut cluster event, parameterized by the Node if present or null if absent.
definitions.usage_context: Trigger_event in SP8.

definitions.definition: PREDICATE/EVENT. Represents that the Fabric Admin has revoked access to the Content App either for a specific Node (if provided) or for all non-Admin Nodes when node is null.
definitions.usage_context: Consequence in SP8.

references.name: ClusterRevision
references.description: Global attribute storing cluster revision; used to differentiate behavior such as 8-character PIN support but not explicitly modeled in FSM.

references.name: PASE (password-authenticated session establishment)
references.description: Secure session setup protocol that uses the SetupPIN returned by GetSetupPIN when accounts match; cryptographic details are outside this FSM.

references.name: Central Account Mapping Service
references.description: External service that maps Temporary Account Identifier to accounts and Setup PINs and enforces aggressive timeouts; abstracted by temp_id_expired and setup_pin_valid predicates.

references.name: Fabric Admin
references.description: Responsible for revoking access to the Content App in response to LoggedOut events; behavior captured by security property SP8.

references.name: MatterCore - Manual Pairing Code
references.description: Defines the Setup PIN used in PASE; this FSM treats SetupPIN as an opaque string subject to length and validity constraints.
